rulings.Case Title: BETTY WILLIAMS, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR et al VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 75, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Defendant Autozone West, Inc.S Notice Of Motiona And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Wrongful Death (Asbestos)
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 21, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Autozone West, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ first amended complaint is denied in part and granted in part. Defendant¿s motion is denied as to the substance of plaintiffs¿ complaint. Plaintiffs¿ complaint alleges facts sufficient to plead a claim for punitive damages. Defendant¿s motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity to clarify which plaintiff(s) is/are making a claim for punitive damages. Punitive damages are not awardable to the heirs on their own cause of action for wrongful death. (C.C.P. 377.34, 377.61.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ALFRED DELFINO VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 21, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden because it demonstrated that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant was not obligated to address any person¿s alleged knowledge in support of plaintiff¿s claims other than plaintiff himself, as defendant¿s special interrogatories asked plaintiff to identify all witnesses with information to support his claims and plaintiff¿s responses thereto, which were served after plaintiff¿s deposition, did not identify Henry Goring, George Goring or anyone else. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Goring¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s initial declaration based on lack of signature are sustained. (C.C.P. 2015.5.) Further, although plaintiff subsequently served a signed version of Mr. Ay¿s declaration, such submission was untimely and was not considered by the court because plaintiff failed to demonstrate good cause for the late evidence. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: SIDNEY D. BAGGETT et al VS. ASBESTOS COMPANIES
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MARCH 21, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3, Defendant Ingersoll Rand Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Baggett was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The statements in Mr. Baggett¿s deposition testimony and declaration that the pumps at Harvey¿s had never been worked on are inadmissible hearsay and were not considered by the court. Defendant¿s objections to the remainder of Mr. Baggett¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s initial declaration based on lack of signature are sustained. (C.C.P. 2015.5.) However the court did consider the signed version of Mr. Ay¿s declaration because plaintiffs demonstrated good cause for the late evidence. (See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019).) Although the court considered it, Mr. Ay¿s declaration is not admissible to create a triable issue whether any of the packing and/or gaskets at issue aboard the USS Belleau Wood contained asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
